required_skills: Defence systems architecture design and system-of-systems (SoS) integration
Model-based systems engineering (MBSE) and SysML for defence capability definition
Requirements engineering, decomposition, and traceability across complex defence programmes
System verification and validation (V&V) planning and execution per MIL-STD-499 or DEF STAN 00-056
Defence acquisition lifecycle management (CADMID or DoD 5000.02 acquisition framework)
Electronic warfare (EW), command and control (C2), and ISR system integration
Interface management and interface control document (ICD) development for multi-domain systems
Risk management and hazard analysis using MIL-STD-882 system safety standard
NATO STANAG interoperability standards and coalition force integration
Reliability, availability, maintainability, and testability (RAMT) analysis for defence platforms
preferred_skills: Experience with UKMOD or US DoD defence acquisition and contracting processes
Knowledge of cyber security standards for defence systems (JSP 440, NIST SP 800-53)
Familiarity with human factors engineering and human-machine interface (HMI) design for defence
Experience with directed energy weapons (DEW) or hypersonic weapons system integration
Knowledge of modelling and simulation (M&S) environments for operational analysis and wargaming
Familiarity with EW threat modelling and radar cross-section (RCS) signature management
Experience with through-life capability management (TLCM) and in-service support engineering
responsibilities: Define and maintain defence system architectures using MBSE and SysML, decomposing top-level operational requirements into system, subsystem, and component-level specifications
Lead system-of-systems integration planning across platform, sensor, weapon, and C2 domains, managing cross-system interfaces and resolving interoperability conflicts between stakeholders
Develop and execute system verification and validation plans, coordinating ground integration tests, hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) trials, and live firing demonstrations against KPIs
Conduct MIL-STD-882 system safety assessments including preliminary hazard analysis (PHA), subsystem hazard analysis (SSHA), and system hazard analysis (SHA) for defence platforms
Manage requirements lifecycle in IBM DOORS from operational capability statement through system specification to subsystem ICD, maintaining full bidirectional traceability
Collaborate with programme managers and defence customers to translate operational concepts (CONOPs) and user needs into verified, testable technical performance measures (TPMs)
Perform trade studies and operational analysis to evaluate system architecture options against cost, schedule, technical risk, and operational effectiveness criteria
Ensure NATO STANAG and coalition interoperability compliance by defining data link, communication, and interface standards for multinational force integration
Lead reliability, availability, maintainability, and testability (RAMT) analyses and drive design improvements to meet defence platform availability and readiness requirements
Support defence acquisition milestone reviews (Preliminary Design Review, Critical Design Review, System Qualification Review) by preparing compliance evidence and technical baseline documentation
